name: Remotion Assembly Adapter
purpose: Turn approved TV shots, subtitles, voice and sound packages into a TV-feeling 9:16 MP4 using React-based video rendering.
why_remotion: The dashboard is already React-based, so timeline components can reuse the same thinking.
Captions, still frames, motion clips, overlays and audio can be rendered in one deterministic composition.
It is cleaner for TV-feeling assembly than manually hand-writing huge FFmpeg filter graphs.
It can coexist with FFmpeg: Remotion handles layout/timeline, FFmpeg remains fallback and utility layer.
render_layers: subtle camera move when only still frame exists
intro/outro sting only after story is readable
implementation_steps: Create Remotion project folder after keyframes are approved.
Map assembly package timeline into Remotion Sequence components.
Load subtitles from the generated voice package or SRT file.
Render a preview MP4 to outputs/pilot/exports.
Only then add npm dependencies for Remotion.
do_not_do_yet: Do not replace the dashboard with Remotion.
Do not install heavy Remotion dependencies until the keyframe pipeline is clean.
Do not burn subtitles into generated images; subtitles are a video layer only.
first_render_goal: A rough but watchable 60-second vertical pilot using still keyframes, small zooms, voice, sound cues and clean subtitles.
